title: GoFundMe launched for Mass. family after teen dies in crash from overpass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travis Teixeira, 18, from Ashburnham, died after his car fell from a Route 2 overpass in Leominster, Massachusetts.
A GoFundMe campaign was launched by a relative to support Travis Teixeira's family, raising over $70,000 for expenses.
The GoFundMe aims to cover funeral costs and support family members taking time off work to grieve.

title: ‘Stay strong’: David Ortiz’s defiant declaration in the wake of tragedy still resonates with Boston
author: Chad Finn
bullets: David Ortiz addressed Boston on April 20, 2013, after the Marathon bombings, delivering a message of resilience.
Ortiz thanked city leaders during a pregame ceremony, emphasizing the collective efforts after the tragic event.
His declaration 'this is our city' became a rallying cry for Boston, resonating with citizens during a tough time.

title: Holy Cross student missing after canoe capsizes in Connecticut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Holy Cross student Dominick Tucci, 20, is missing after a canoe capsized on Lake Hayward in East Haddam, Connecticut.
A Good Samaritan rescued Tucci's friend, who was hospitalized and later discharged; Tucci remains unaccounted for.
Search and rescue operations, including dive teams and aerial surveillance, were suspended Sunday and slated to resume Monday.

title: The unsolved murder of Kendric Price, a beloved youth mentor gunned down on his childhood street in Dorchester
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Kendric Price, a youth mentor and financial analyst, was killed in March 2019 on his childhood street in Dorchester.
Price, founder of Big Business Network nonprofit, coached basketball and mentored youth using sports to teach finances.
Boston police continue to investigate the unsolved murder, urging anyone with information to contact homicide detectives.

title: The basement of this $4.7m Lynnfield home known for its holiday decor is like a private club
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: 1 Haywood Farm, a 8,190-square-foot home in Lynnfield, is listed for $4.695 million by The Sarkis Team.
The basement features amenities like an eight-person bar, screening room, sauna, and a kids' playroom.
The property is known for elaborate holiday decor, drawing thousands during events like Halloween.

title: Patriots reportedly set to release QB Joshua Dobbs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots plan to release QB Joshua Dobbs, who was a backup to Drake Maye last season.
Dobbs, 31, signed a two-year deal in 2025 and played in four games with 65 passing yards.
Re-signing quarterback Tommy DeVito may have influenced the decision to release Dobbs from the Patriots.

title: Tracy McGrady questions whether Celtics are ‘still on same the page’ following Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tracy McGrady questions Boston Celtics' team cohesion following Jayson Tatum's return from Achilles injury.
Celtics' shooting percentages have decreased since Tatum's return; they shot 36% against Minnesota and 45% against Memphis.
Carmelo Anthony suggests more low-post play for Tatum, who has taken fewer than a third of his shots inside 10 feet this season.

title: One New Englander is in this year’s class at Boston’s Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame: Tom Rush
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Tom Rush, a Portsmouth, N.H. native, is inducted into the Folk Americana Roots Hall of Fame in Boston.
Rush joins fellow musicians like Neil Young and Jackson Browne in the 2026 induction class of living artists.
Tom Rush will perform at the sold-out ceremony along with Judy Collins and his new accompanist, Brendan Cleary.

title: Jaylen Brown sheds light on 1-on-1 meeting with Jayson Tatum ahead of playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum had a productive meeting before Tatum's return from a 10-month injury rehab.
The Celtics leaders discussed improving team communication and setting goals ahead of the upcoming playoffs.
Boston, second in the Eastern Conference, will face the Knicks at Madison Square Garden on April 9th.

title: Livestream: Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial gets underway
author: John Waller
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, is on trial for assault with a dangerous weapon, pleading not guilty.
Fitzsimmons was shot by a colleague in her home last summer, leading to the current charges.
The trial is being livestreamed by NBC10 Boston, as reported by Boston.com Deputy Editor John Waller.
